mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-increase episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows, or GDP growth episodes coincide with negative equity returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth surpassing 0.5% month-over-month signals strong consumer demand that immediately boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in backtesting.
